title: pyrus × parnassica
intro: Pyrus × parnassica, also known as the Pyrus pashia or the Bitter Cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic. The health benefits of Pyrus × parnassica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various ailments such as rheumatism and arthritis, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antipyretic. In traditional herbal medicine, the bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also sometimes dried and used as a powder or tincture.
